title: History of the Internet
content: The internet originated in the 1960s with the creation of ARPANET, funded by the U.S. Department of Defense. ARPANET successfully implemented packet switching, a revolutionary technology for data transfer. In 1983, the network adopted the TCP/IP protocol suite, establishing the foundation of the modern internet. Tim Berners-Lee invented the World Wide Web in 1989 while working at CERN. The Web made the internet accessible to the general public through web browsers and hyperlinks. By the late 1990s, the dot-com boom saw rapid commercialization. Today, the internet connects billions of devices worldwide and is fundamental to global communication, commerce, and entertainment.
